items.title: RM150mil allocation for Mitra a historic moment for Indian community - Ramanan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The boost in the allocation for the Malaysian Indian Community Transformation Unit (Mitra) to RM150 million, from RM100 million previously, marks a historic moment for the Indian community in the country. Formerly known as SEDIC, Mitra is a special unit tasked with addressing the socioeconomic development of the Indian community in Malaysia. Ramanan said he deeply appreciates the Madani government's commitment to empowering the Indian community through various socioeconomic development initiatives.

items.title: Kuching mayor: Public anger understandable, but illegal to dump carcass of dog that attacked jogger into river
items.summary: KUCHING, May 10 — The disposal of the carcass of a dog reported to have attacked a woman while she was jogging at a park in Stutong last Friday into a river is an offence under local cleanliness by-laws, said Kuching South Mayor Dato Wee Hong Seng. Wee said although public anger over the incident was understandable, disposing of the carcass into a river remained against the law. “A normal dog would not behave like that, especially towards the person feeding it,” he said.

items.title: Sabah business groups back say bringing back barter trade could boost local economies
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 10 — Several of Sabah’s leading trade and maritime associations have voiced strong support for Chief Minister Datuk Seri Panglima Hajiji Noor’s proposal to revive barter trade along the state’s eastern maritime borders and northern Kudat region. “Formalising and regulating barter trade will further optimise our port facilities,” he said. KCCI Trade and Investment representative Carl V Moosom, who previously led the BIMP-EAGA Business Council Sabah, said reopening regulated barter trade routes would generate positive economic spillover effects for local districts.

items.title: Shooting incident: Tan Lek Khang regains consciousness, still weak
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Former Johor Bersatu associate chief Datuk Tan Lek Khang, who was seriously injured after allegedly shooting himself in the chest on April 28, has regained consciousness. His friend and former party information chief, Peter Khoo Kong Ek, confirmed that Tan was still undergoing treatment at a hospital here and might require further follow-up surgery. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad was previously reported as saying that Tan was believed to have accidentally discharged his firearm while unloading it after a shooting practice session at the Ladang Rem Shooting Range in Kota Tinggi.

items.title: Iran army says countries siding with US to 'face difficulties' in Hormuz
items.summary: TEHRAN: Iran's army warned on Sunday that countries complying with US sanctions against the Islamic republic would face difficulties crossing the Strait of Hormuz. Early this month the US government imposed new sanctions on Iranian interests and warned ships against paying authorities in Tehran to pass through the strait. "Countries that comply with the United States by imposing sanctions on the Islamic Republic of Iran will certainly face difficulties crossing the strait," army official Mohammad Akraminia told Iran's official IRNA news agency on Sunday.

items.title: No Bersatu outcast applied to join Pas despite remaining pro-PN
items.summary: KOTA BARU: None of the elected representatives or leaders sacked or suspended by Bersatu has sought to join Pas despite remaining within Perikatan Nasional (PN). Pas secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan said the party had not received any application or request from the Bersatu outcasts. "To us, this (the suspension and sacking of Bersatu leaders) is a Bersatu issue," Takiyuddin said when contacted, declining further comment.

items.title: Anwar calls on youth to reject racial politics, embrace unity
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim wants the younger generation to reject narrow racial sentiments, saying they must play a central role in shaping a more harmonious and stable Malaysia. He said young Malaysians should emerge as a unifying force that transcends race and religion, rather than inheriting divisive attitudes from previous generations. This country cannot continue to tolerate these excesses," he said at a dialogue session with Indian students at Universiti Malaya.

items.title: AADK detects over 1,000 drug-positive cases at Malaysia border entry points since 2024
items.summary: He said in 2024, a total of 494 individuals were arrested after testing positive for drugs involving 444 men and 50 women with methamphetamine recording the highest number of cases at 148 cases, followed by ketamine (109 cases), tetrahydrocannabinol (THC) or cannabis (96 cases), ‘4-methylenedioxymethamphetamine’ (MDMA) (80 cases), benzo (62 cases), ketum (23 cases) and opiates (four cases). “Last year, a total of 415 people were arrested, involving 392 men and 23 women, with the highest drug recorded being methamphetamine in 140 cases, followed by ketamine (108 cases), THC (70 cases), benzo (59 cases), MDMA (31 cases), opiates (five cases) and ketum in two cases. “A total of 175 individuals aged 18 to 60 were arrested this year until March 31, involving 167 men and eight women, with the highest drug recorded being methamphetamine in 65 cases, followed by ketamine (43 cases), THC (32 cases), MDMA (22 cases) and ketum (13 cases),” he told reporters after the 2026 Drug-Free Aspiration Tour: Gombak-Kita Turun Padang Aspiration Tour in Kampung Kemensah, Ulu Klang, here, today.

items.title: New Zealand pedestrian injured in Chow Kit motorcycle crash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 49-year-old New Zealand pedestrian was injured after being hit by a motorcycle at Jalan Chow Kit early this morning. City police Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department (TEID) chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the department received a report on the incident, which occurred at about 2.37am. "Preliminary investigations found that the accident involved a Yamaha Y15ZR motorcycle ridden by a 26-year-old local man and a 49-year-old male pedestrian from New Zealand.

items.title: New Zealand tourist breaks leg after being hit by motorcycle in Chow Kit jaywalking incident
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 10 — A New Zealand tourist suffered a broken leg after he was hit by a motorcycle while allegedly jaywalking along Chow Kit Road heading towards Sogo here early this morning. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT) chief ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said police received a report on the accident at about 2.37 am, involving a Yamaha Y15ZR motorcycle ridden by a 26-year-old local man and a 49-year-old male tourist from New Zealand. “As a result of the accident, the motorcyclist suffered minor injuries to his hands and legs, while the pedestrian sustained a fractured right leg, injuries to the back of his head and swelling on his right hand, and is receiving treatment at Kuala Lumpur Hospital,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Kommuniti Madani must be govt’s eyes and ears at grassroots level, says Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 10 — Kommuniti Madani must play an active role as the “eyes, ears, tongue, feet and hands” of the government in ensuring that every initiative and assistance reaches the targeted groups directly at the grassroots level, said Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. He said appointed Kommuniti Madani members carry a huge responsibility to be on the ground to listen to the people’s grievances and explain state and federal government policies in the simplest manner possible. “Convey the information so that immediate intervention can be carried out every week,” he said when speaking at the presentation ceremony of appointment letters for Kommuniti Madani Kuala Lumpur and Selangor here today.

items.title: Proposed ombudsman to boost public confidence in complaints process - Azalina
items.summary: BANGI: The proposed Malaysian Ombudsman is aimed at addressing public perceptions over the transparency of internal complaint processes and does not mean existing mechanisms are ineffective. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said the Public Complaints Bureau (BPA) is not ineffective but it is subject to public perceptions concerning the transparency and outcomes of complaints because it operates within the government's administrative structure. "The ombudsman will separate the Public Complaints Bureau from government agencies.

items.title: Lorry in fatal crash probe catches fire outside Kuala Pilah police HQ
items.summary: KUALA PILAH: A lorry involved in a fatal accident investigation caught fire outside the Kuala Pilah police headquarters early this morning. District police chief Superintendent Muhamad Mustafah Hussin said police were alerted to the fire at 2.45am. "The accident involved the lorry and two cars, and resulted in one death," he said.

items.title: Malaysia tightens border health checks as hantavirus alert raised, says Dzulkefly
items.summary: Health Minister Datuk Seri Dzulkefly Ahmad said the approach is crucial as public health challenges are now global in nature, and no country is truly safe without continuous precautionary measures. In addition, border control measures at all international entry points have been tightened through health screenings and heightened preparedness at airports, seaports and major gateways by the International Entry Point Health Office. “These measures are ongoing to ensure that vessels entering the country comply with public health requirements and sanitation,” he said.

items.title: Ministers extend Mother's Day wishes, honour sacrifices of mothers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Cabinet members, including Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof, today shared Mother's Day greetings and messages of appreciation through their respective social media platforms to honour the sacrifices, love and dedication of mothers. "Happy Mother's Day. "A mother's sincere love never changes.

items.title: Health Ministry launches Lung Shield programme to screen 10,000 traffic cops for lung cancer
items.summary: CYBERJAYA: The Health Ministry has launched a large-scale Lung Shield programme aimed at screening 10,000 traffic police personnel over the next three years in a bid to detect lung cancer at its earliest stages through advanced AI-assisted technology. Officiating the launch during the 2026 World No Tobacco Day celebration, Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad described the initiative as a key milestone in Malaysia's journey towards a smoke-free future. The programme will employ advanced diagnostic tools, including artificial intelligence (AI)-assisted chest X-rays and low-dose CT scans, to identify lung abnormalities at the earliest possible stage.

items.title: Vijay becomes Tamil Nadu's new chief minister
items.summary: HYDERABAD: Film star-turned-politician C. Joseph Vijay was sworn in as Tamil Nadu state's new chief minister on Sunday after he was promised support by other parties to achieve the required legislative majority. His father S.A. Chandrasekhar, mother Shobha, Tamil actress Trisha Krishnan and Congress party leader Rahul Gandhi were present. Vijay promised a transparent government and better law and order in the state of 77 million.

items.title: Anwar extends Mother's Day wishes, expresses appreciation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today extended Mother's Day greetings to all mothers, expressing appreciation for their sacrifices, love and unwavering devotion. "Happy Mother's Day!" This year's national -level Mother's Day celebration carries the theme "Seteguh Kasih Ibu".

items.title: Healthcare drag clouds Pentamaster outlook
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pentamaster Corp Bhd's earnings are expected to face near-term pressure from its newly-commercialised healthcare segment, according to Phillip Capital Sdn Bhd. The firm said outstanding order book was supported by medical (55 per cent) and consumer and industrial products (24 per cent), followed by automotive (11 per cent), opto-electronics (6.0 per cent) and others (5 per cent). Phillip Capital cut its FY26-FY27 earnings forecasts on Pentamaster between eight per cent and 24 per cent, reflecting deeper-than-expected losses from the healthcare segment.

items.title: Ayob Khan reiterates no police links to Ketereh murder suspect’s family, warns against misinformation
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 10 — Deputy Inspector General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay has reiterated that claims linking the father of a suspect in the Ketereh college student murder case to the police are false. According to Harian Metro , Ayob Khan said the allegation had already been clarified by several parties. He said explanations had already been issued by the Kelantan police chief, the Kota Bharu district police chief and Bukit Aman.

items.title: Police await MCMC report in probe over alleged insult to Sultan of Selangor
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Police are awaiting a forensic analysis report from the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) before referring the investigation paper involving a man accused of insulting the Sultan of Selangor on social media to the Attorney-General's Chambers (A-GC). Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay said the man was arrested on May 3 and remanded for three days until May 6 to assist investigations. Ayob said the man had been released after his remand period ended, pending completion of the investigation.

items.title: Border urine tests net over 1,000 drug-positive travellers, says Nada
items.summary: "In 2024, 494 people tested positive, comprising 444 men and 50 women. "For 2025, 415 people were detained, involving 392 men and 23 women. Methamphetamine recorded 65 cases, followed by ketamine (43), THC (32), MDMA (22) and ketum (13).

items.title: Miracle twins after 12-year wait: A Mother's Day story of hope and resilience
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A mother's 12-year wait for children was finally rewarded with the birth of twins following numerous fertility treatments, a journey for which she was honoured with the Special Mother's Inspiration Award during the National Mother's Day celebrations today. Police officer Assistant Superintendent Nurul Zaanizila Mohd Zain, 44, said the long journey to motherhood is one filled with immense challenges. "I tried various traditional and modern treatments.

items.title: Couple held over RM800,000 Taman Melawati break-in
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A married couple has been detained in connection with a house break-in at Taman Melawati that resulted in the loss of jewellery and watches worth about RM800,000. Ampang Jaya district police chief sssistant commissioner Khairul Anuar Khalid said the Indonesian couple was arrested yesterday. Police also found three fingerprint impressions at the scene to assist in investigations.

items.title: Tuanku Zara graces Mother's Day celebration, spotlight on support for mothers
items.summary: Her Royal Highness was received upon arrival by the Prime Minister's wife, Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, and Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri. In her speech, Nancy said the government remained committed to reinforcing support systems for women and families through a comprehensive and inclusive approach centred on strengthening the family institution. "Through comprehensive fertility services at affordable rates, including treatment subsidies, we have managed more than 373,000 subfertility cases, with 6,513 successful pregnancies," she said.

items.title: World deprived of 1 billion barrels of oil over past two months, Aramco CEO says
items.summary: DUBAI: The world has lost about one billion barrels of oil over the past two months and energy markets will take time to stabilise even if flows resume, Saudi oil giant Saudi Aramco chief executive officer Amin Nasser said on Sunday, as shipping disruptions choke traffic through the Strait of Hormuz. Global energy supplies have been sharply squeezed by Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, which has curtailed shipping and driven prices higher following the US-Israeli war. Despite shifts in shipping routes, Nasser reiterated that Asia remained a key priority for the company and was central to global demand.

items.title: PM Anwar confirms RON95 aid review, stresses income brackets must not be wrongly affected
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 10 — The government is currently reviewing RON95 assistance for upper income earners, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today, amid growing calls for petrol subsidies to be reserved for the lower- and middle-income groups. But any recalibration of petrol aid would possibly avoid touching upper middle-income earners too, Anwar suggested. Anwar said the government has “agreed in principle” to the proposal to exclude or partially assist top income earners from getting subsidised petrol.

items.title: Some agencies push back against ombudsman plan, Azalina says
items.summary: Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said negotiations are ongoing to bring these agencies on board. she said. she said.

items.title: Amendments to act will ensure Mara stays on course despite govt change — Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Proposed amendments to the Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) Act are aimed at ensuring the agency's policies and governance remain consistent even when governments change, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said today. Zahid, who is also Rural and Regional Development Minister, said the amendments would institutionalise governance standards within Mara and strengthen accountability mechanisms across the agency. "The prime minister instructed Mara to prepare the amendments and new act within three months.

items.title: Lung cancer crisis worsening as 95pct cases detected too late, says Dzulkefly
items.summary: CYBERJAYA: An estimated 95 per cent of lung cancer cases in Malaysia are detected only at Stage 3 or 4, contributing to a low five-year relative survival rate of about 11 per cent, according to Malaysian Council for Tobacco Control (MCTC). As a scientist by background, I understand all too well that there is no safe level of exposure to tobacco smoke," he said during the national-level World No Tobacco Day 2026 celebration and the National Tobacco Control Conference and Lung Health Summit 2026 today. Data from the Global Adult Tobacco Survey (GATS) Malaysia 2023 showed that 19.5 per cent of adults, or about 4.8 million people, currently use tobacco products, while 5.8 per cent, equivalent to 1.4 million adults, use electronic cigarettes.

items.title: MetMalaysia warns of thunderstorms across 10 states, including KL, until 4pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds affecting several states in Peninsular Malaysia and the Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur until 4pm today. Also affected are Negeri Sembilan (Jelebu), Selangor (Hulu Selangor, Gombak, Petaling and Hulu Langat) as well as Johor (Segamat, Kluang, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Baru). In addition, Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Raub and Bentong), Perak (Kerian, Larut Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang and Muallim) and Kelantan (Tumpat, Pasir Mas and Tanah Merah) are also involved.– BERNAMA © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: No hantavirus cases reported in Malaysia, surveillance stepped up
items.summary: Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said the ministry is maintaining a high level of preparedness through global monitoring and strict screening at all international entry points. "It is crucial that we conduct health screenings on ships and vessels entering our waters, particularly those arriving from high-risk areas," Dzulkefly said. The minister said the ministry has issued 2,421 pratique certificates and 1,146 ship sanitation certificates following these inspections.

items.title: Ayob Khan: Claims linking suspect's father to police are false
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay said that claims linking a suspect's father in the college student murder case to police are false. Ayob's remarks come after Kelantan police dismissed viral claims that the father of a teenage suspect in the Ketereh murder case is a retired policeman with " strong connections ". Ayob said police are waiting for instructions on the next course of action.

items.title: 'Others only talked, Anwar acted': Azalina commends PM for pursuing tenure limit law
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: While many past prime ministers have discussed limiting the tenure of the nation's top office, Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is the only one who has taken concrete steps to make it a reality. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said that Anwar should be commended for willingly subjecting his own tenure to such a limitation. "To be very honest, if you were the prime minister, would you want to limit your power?

items.title: Musyawarah Nasional to be registered as an NGO - Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Musyawarah Nasional platform will be formally registered as a non-governmental organisation, with the Registrar of Societies (ROS) Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said today. "The Permanent Secretariat of Musyawarah Nasional should take immediate action to register Musyawarah Nasional as a non-governmental organisation under the Registrar of Societies (ROS), Malaysia. Zahid, who founded the platform said he wanted Musyawarah Nasional to become an organised, legitimate, credible and continuous platform.

items.title: Abang Johari says Sarawak to acquire more federal assets to boost economy and create high‑tech jobs
items.summary: KUCHING, May 10 — Sarawak plans to take over more strategic entities and assets of the federal government to strengthen the state’s economy in line with the Post-Covid-19 Development Strategy 2030 (PCDS 2030), said Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg. He said the move was important in expanding the state’s economic dominance, thus creating more highly skilled job opportunities for Sarawakians, especially in the technology and new industry sectors. Abang Johari said Sarawak’s economic transformation is currently moving towards a high-tech economy that focuses on renewable energy, automation, artificial intelligence (AI), digitalisation and the semiconductor industry.

items.title: [UPDATED] Govt to finalise proposed review of T20 fuel subsidies soon
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim says the proposal for more comprehensive targeted subsidies, particularly the rationalisation of RON95 fuel subsidies for the T20 income group, is still being refined and is expected to be finalised soon. He said the proposal was currently being reviewed by the Crisis Management Task Force under the National Economic Action Council (NEAC), chaired by Tan Sri Mohd Hassan Marican. Akmal proposes redirecting fuel subsidies to B40 and M40 groups.

items.title: Sri Lanka to hike electricity tariff amid energy crisis
items.summary: COLOMBO: Sri Lanka will increase electricity rates by up to 18 per cent from Monday to offset the additional costs of generating power using thermal plants due to the Middle East war, the Public Utilities Commission said. The latest hike comes on top of a 40 per cent tariff increase introduced last month. Sri Lanka has also raised fuel prices by more than 35 per cent and rationed the same following energy supply disruptions.

items.title: Teo Nie Ching encourages individuals facing social media restrictions to appeal through tribunal chaired by judge
items.summary: KULAI, May 10 — Individuals facing social media account restrictions or content removal are advised to file an appeal through the Communications, Multimedia and Online Safety Appeal Tribunal. Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching said the tribunal is chaired by a High Court judge together with at least two tribunal members. “This is an ad hoc appeal tribunal that is neither chaired nor heard by officers of the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), but by a High Court judge,” she told reporters after handing over aid to outstanding Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) 2025 students here today.

items.title: Zahid proposes mandatory full-time halal executives for companies holding Jakim certification
items.summary: KUAL LUMPUR: All companies holding halal certification from the Department of Islamic Development Malaysia (Jakim) should be required to appoint a full-time halal executive to ensure strict compliance with halal standards, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said today. Zahid, who is also chairman of the National Halal Council, said the proposal was aimed at strengthening preventive mechanisms rather than relying solely on enforcement after breaches occur. Speaking at the closing session of the Musyawarah Nasional titled "Malay Concerns: What Are the Solutions?, he said the current approach of revoking halal certification only after violations is no longer sufficient.

items.title: Addressing Indian students, Anwar says ‘stand to be corrected’ over temple row remarks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 10 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today appeared to acknowledge that some of his remarks about Hindu temples were perceived as tacit support for hostility directed at the Indian community. Speaking to Indian students at Universiti Malaya here, Anwar suggested that his call for stern enforcement against houses of worship built without permits may have been “misconstrued”, describing such interpretations as attempts to sow division. “In many Western countries, the far right is winning because they exploit racial sentiments,” Anwar said during a government programme here.

items.title: Police ramp up patrols and roadblocks in Durian Tunggal after spate of armed robberies
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH, May 10 — Police will step up crime prevention patrols and roadblocks around Durian Tunggal following a recent spate of house break-ins and armed robberies in the area. Alor Gajah police chief Supt Ahmad Abu Bakar said these measures will be carried out regularly to ease residents’ fears and boost their confidence in the police’s ability to maintain public safety and order. He added that the operation involved four officers and 30 personnel from the Alor Gajah District Police Headquarters and the Durian Tunggal Police Station.

items.title: Ministers pay tribute to mothers on Mother’s Day, highlight sacrifices and strength of family institution
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 10 — Members of the Cabinet, including Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof, today shared Mother’s Day greetings and messages of appreciation through their respective social media platforms to honour the sacrifices, love and dedication of mothers. “Happy Mother’s Day. “A mother’s sincere love never changes.

items.title: Umno open to Political Funding Bill, but official stance still being finalised, says Azalina
items.summary: BANGI, May 10 — Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said Umno has no objection to the proposed drafting of the Political Funding Bill. The Umno Information chief said, however, the party has yet to formally present its official position as the process of gathering feedback on the proposed act from all political parties is still ongoing. Previously, Azalina said that the proposed Political Funding Act is still under study and requires the consensus of all political parties, as they will be the main users of the legislation.

items.title: Malaysia records 556 fuel and LPG offences under Ops Tiris 4.0, RM23m in seizures made
items.summary: Of the total cases recorded, diesel-related offences accounted for the highest number at 249 cases, followed by petrol with 119 cases. “Overall, Sarawak recorded the highest number of diesel-related cases, followed by Sabah and Johor. “Meanwhile, Sabah also recorded the highest number of LPG-related cases, followed by Kedah.

items.title: Man killed, another injured in Skudai entertainment outlet shooting as Johor police hunt woman believed to be shooter
items.summary: JOHOR BARU, May 10 — Police have confirmed that a man was killed and another injured in a shooting outside an entertainment outlet in Skudai late Saturday night. Iskandar Puteri district police chief ACP M. Kumarasan said the incident took place at Taman Industri Jaya around 11pm and involved a local woman, aged 27, believed to be the shooter. “One local male victim, aged 27, died at the scene, while another man of the same age sustained injuries after being shot,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Zahid calls for firm action against insults on Islam and Malay Rulers to safeguard national harmony
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 10 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has called for insults against Islam and the institution of the Malay Rulers to be dealt with firmly, fairly and wisely, to protect religious dignity, state institutions and national harmony. He said unease among the Malay community is deepening as the sanctity of Islam and the standing of the Malay Rulers are increasingly being challenged through various issues that stir public sensitivities. “Islam is the religion of the Federation, and the Malay Rulers are the pillars of national sovereignty.

items.title: Two men remanded over alleged murder of PwD victim in Bachok assault case
items.summary: PASIR PUTEH, May 10 — Two men, both aged 25, have been remanded for six days to assist in investigations into the death of a person with disabilities (PwD) at Ladang Merdeka Cherbu, Padang Lati, Bachok, three weeks ago. The remand order was issued by Magistrate Nik Mohd Fadli Datuk Nik Azlan today, allowing police to detain the suspects until next Friday for investigations under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder. It was previously reported that a 20-year-old man with learning disabilities died after he was allegedly assaulted in an incident at Ladang Merdeka Cherbu, Padang Lati, Bachok, on April 15.

items.title: Twenty‑four Chinese citizens deported after immigration raids in Semporna
items.summary: SEMPORNA, May 10 — Twenty-four Chinese citizens were deported after they were detained during immigration operations in Semporna last month. Sabah Immigration Director Sh Sitti Saleha Habib Yussof said the deportation followed arrests made during Ops Kutip, Ops Mahir and Ops Sapu on April 21. “They were investigated under Regulation 39(b) of the Immigration Regulations 1963 and Section 15(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63,” she said in a statement, Saturday.

items.title: Wangsa Maju district police confirm report of fatal fall in Jalan Ampang
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, May 10 — Police have confirmed that a person fell from a tower block in Jalan Ampang yesterday, with the case classified as sudden death. In a brief statement, Wangsa Maju district police said: “We have received a report regarding the incident at a tower in Jalan Ampang.” A video of the incident has since circulated widely on social media, with users claiming it took place around 1pm. * If you are lonely, distressed, or having negative thoughts, Befrienders offers free and confidential support 24 hours a day.

items.title: Single mother loses RM60,500 in love scam after falling for TikTok romance ruse
items.summary: District police chief ACP Azli Mohd Noor said the victim, in her late 30s, became acquainted with a male suspect on TikTok before continuing their communication via WhatsApp. He said that after several days of getting to know each other, the suspect asked the victim to transfer RM500, purportedly for hotel expenses. He said the victim realised she had been scammed after attempts to contact the suspect several days later failed.

items.title: Johor immigration nabs 27 foreign workers in food premises raids over permit offences
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 10 — The Immigration Department detained 27 foreign nationals for various offences in an operation targeting food premises in the district from 3 pm yesterday. Johor Immigration director Datuk Mohd Rusdi Mohd Darus said they comprised 18 men and six women from Myanmar, as well as one man each from Bangladesh, Pakistan and Nepal, aged between 19 and 46. “The restaurant waiters and cooks are suspected of committing offences under the Immigration Act 1959/63 and the Immigration Regulations 1963, and have been placed at the Setia Tropika Immigration Depot for further investigation,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: GOF, Thai authorities to hold border security talks in Krabi over illegal immigration issue
items.summary: GOF Southeast Brigade commander SAC Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the meeting would serve as a platform to strengthen cooperation and intelligence sharing in efforts to curb cross-border criminal activities, including human smuggling. Meanwhile, Ahmad Radzi said the GOF Southeast Brigade recorded 51 cases of illegal immigrants through OTWK from Jan 1 to May 8, involving 318 individuals, with Myanmar nationals making up the highest number at 227 people. He also said syndicates are now detected using sea and river routes around Tumpat to smuggle in immigrants, involving land coordinators from several other states, while the GOF Eastern Brigade has also recorded seizures worth RM195 million since January.

items.title: TTDI residents demand closure of KLGCC back road, buffer zone around Bukit Kiara in petition to DBKL
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 10 — The Taman Tun Dr Ismail Residents Association (TTDIRA) and environmental group Friends of Bukit Kiara (FoBK) have submitted 2,458 signed petitions to Kuala Lumpur Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud, calling for the closure of a back road from the Kuala Lumpur Golf and Country Club (KLGCC) into TTDI and the creation of a no development buffer zone around Bukit Kiara. TTDIRA chairman Abu Hafiz said the community feared traffic volumes from KLGCC’s resort projects would overwhelm TTDI’s narrow roads. FoBK secretary Clinton Ang warned of ecological damage if development encroached on Bukit Kiara.

items.title: Anthony Loke says central leadership won’t interfere as Sarawak DAP charts own election course
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, May 10 — DAP will press ahead in the coming Sarawak state election, with the party’s central leadership handing full autonomy to its Sarawak chapter to decide candidate line up and campaign strategy. According to Utusan Malaysia , DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke said the central leadership would not interfere in decisions made by the Sarawak chapter, including the number of candidates to be fielded. “DAP Sarawak has full autonomy to make its own decisions on candidates and election strategy,” he told Utusan yesterday, adding that the Sarawak leadership has its own plans based on the state’s political situation.

items.title: Pahang Crown Prince to lead Mahkota Borneo Expedition, climb Mount Kinabalu
items.summary: KUANTAN, May 10 — Tengku Mahkota Pahang Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri’ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah has consented to lead the Mahkota Borneo Expedition scheduled to take place from May 11 to 13. He said that through the expedition, His Royal Highness will also climb Mount Kinabalu the following day (May 12), accompanied by officers and personnel from the Royal Malaysian Air Force Special Forces (PASKAU) as well as close aides from the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM). “The Mahkota Borneo Expedition also reflects the spirit of leadership, physical endurance and high discipline consistently instilled by His Royal Highness among the younger generation and the nation’s security forces,” he said in a media statement last night.

items.title: Electrocuted Form Three student in Sarawak stabilised, moved out of ICU
items.summary: Matu-Daro district police chief DSP Rahim Mihos said the student was moved yesterday for further treatment. The 15-year-old was one of three students who were electrocuted, including a Form Four student who died, and another student who was unhurt. Meanwhile, the Sarawak state education department posted on social media that the student who died was buried at the Daro Muslim Cemetery on Friday.

items.title: Aaron: Unity Week proving effective platform to foster national harmony
items.summary: National Unity Minister Datuk Aaron Ago Dagang said the signature programme, now approaching its third year of organisation, has successfully created a space for people from diverse backgrounds to gather in one place. The impact of national unity is not measured solely through physical programmes, but also assessed holistically through the Unity Index, which also considers governance and socio-economic development as key indicators, he added. Meanwhile, commenting on the Community Archives Celebration, he said it aims to bring archives closer to society through community involvement in documenting and preserving historical materials and the country’s heritage.

items.title: Woman killed in crash involving SUV, 4WD on Pan Borneo Highway near Sibu
items.summary: SIBU, May 10 — An elderly woman was killed in an accident involving a 4WD vehicle and an SUV at Km17.7 of Jalan Sibu-Selangau along the Pan Borneo Highway last night. Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) Operations Centre spokesman said the 66-year-old victim, who was trapped in the front passenger seat of the SUV, died on the spot. “The woman was found unconscious and still trapped in the front passenger seat, while two other passengers sustained injuries and the driver was unhurt.

items.title: Sultan Ibrahim invites Putin for state visit to Malaysia ahead of diplomatic milestone
items.summary: MOSCOW, May 10 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, yesterday invited Russian President Vladimir Putin to visit Malaysia in 2027, reported Sputnik/RIA Novosti. Relations between Russia and Malaysia are developing well, said Putin. “Our relations between our countries are developing well.
